resources.description: Safe + exposed per resource incl. Crown exposed (§16); keys are CoreResource ONLY (DC6).

buildings.description: BuildingState/queue records land with the Build Queue feature.

workers.description: WorkerState records land with the Economy feature.

claim_ledger.description: Claim Ledger block (Save/Load §16: packages + story claims + remainders) — real shape since A2 (v2).

pending_reward_resolution.description: Persistent pending_reward_resolution records (Doc 04 §10; Save/Load §11; D19/L14) — real shape since A2 (v2).

events.description: Mid-flight event-lifecycle instances (Doc 15 §2; EVT3) — real shape since A3 (v3). Inert staged effects only; no effect execution exists.

expedition.description: Bounded First Playable Expedition Loop domain (brief §2/§3) — real shape since A4 (v4).

harbor_operations.description: Unsafe Overflow holdings + one-time intro/unlock flags (brief §2.12/§2.16/§2.17) — real shape since A4 (v4).

merit.description: Per-faction Merit standing (soulbound; never in 3S bands — DC6/FCT1).

guardian_bond.description: Persistent guardian bond state (GDN10); null until the guardian feature lands.

SaveBlob.description: The complete save blob (Save/Load §16). Field order here is documentation only — serialization is canonical (sorted keys) so round-trips are byte-identical regardless of construction order.

last_saved_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC (§2); reconciliation uses UTC deltas only (§3).

WorldClock.description: In-game World Clock (§2: "day index, time-of-day"). `time_of_day` is an integer pulse counter within the day; its cadence binds to the Economy pulse when the sim core lands (E8 pacing is UNKNOWN until measured).

ResourceBand.description: Per-resource stored state: Safe S + Exposed 2S bands (D1). These are state values; the S/2S/3S caps themselves are /data seed values (DC1), not save fields.

EmptyListM0.description: M0 empty-shell placeholder: schema-enforced empty list until the block's feature + migration land.

ThreatBlock.description: Threat block, minimal at M0: phase only (Doc 05; components land with the threat director).

RaidPhase.description: Threat/raid phase vocabulary (Doc 07 §2; Doc 05 phase order). The empty world's zero-state is "calm"; an Assault never auto-resolves offline (D7).

ClaimLedgerState.description: The Claim Ledger save/sim state (Save/Load §16 `claim_ledger` block: "packages, story claims, remainders" — remainders live inside packages).

is_story.description: Ledger packages are non-story by construction; Story Claims live in their own protected list.

system_grant.description: D13/L13: no system-grant path exists at A2 (literal false until the guarded feature lands).

LedgerPackage.description:
One held (earned-but-unclaimed) reward package in the Claim Ledger (Doc 04 §13; Save/Load §10; Doc 07 §3 `claim_package`).

`contents` is the original as-routed package (immutable record of what was earned); `held_remainder` is what remains unclaimed — entries are strictly positive and never exceed their `contents` counterpart, so at any moment `claimed-so-far == contents − held_remainder` and every partial claim satisfies `claimed + held_remainder == original` (L6). A package with an empty held_remainder is fully claimed and leaves the Ledger.

RewardSourceType.description: The only reward source that exists at A2: test-supplied packages driven by the harness/tests. Gameplay sources (Doc 04 §2) widen this union via migration when their features are authorized and implemented.

RewardPackageContents.description: Reward package contents (Doc 04 §4 / §13 `contents`). At A2 only the resource portion is real; flags/cosmetics/gear_refs are schema-enforced empty (FUTURE BUILD — Doc 04 §4, Doc 09).

RewardResources.description: Per-CoreResource reward amounts. Partial map — only resources actually present carry entries; the generated schema rejects any non-CoreResource key (Merit/XP/BondXP/BondCharge can never be packaged — Doc 04 §3, D26).

EmptyRewardListA2.description: A2 empty-block placeholder (M0 pattern): schema-enforced empty until the feature + migration land.

StoryClaim.description: One protected Story Claim (Doc 04 §6/§11, D15): non-expiring, never deleted, never counts against slot caps. At A2 this is a structural placeholder — Story Claims are created by routing and preserved forever; claiming their resource portion into the Harbor is FUTURE BUILD (no gameplay story source exists to feed them).

blocking_reason.description: Deterministic description of the blocking slot(s), e.g. "per_resource_cap:Crowns" or "global_cap" (Doc 04 §10.6 "naming the blocking slot").

related_system_message_id.description: Literal null until the System Inbox exists (Doc 04A FUTURE BUILD); becomes a message link via migration (M2/M3/DC3).

PendingRewardResolution.description: One persistent pending reward (Doc 04 §10; Save/Load §11; Doc 07 §3; D14/D19/L11/L14): a non-story package that could not enter the Claim Ledger because a slot cap was full. Not in the Ledger, not spendable, never silently deleted, never duplicated by reload. At A2 `pending_id` is the blocked package's `package_id` (1:1, globally unique), preserving exact identity through block → resolve.

PendingResolutionAction.description: The only pending-resolution action implemented at A2: re-deliver the package into the Claim Ledger once slot capacity frees. Doc 04 §10.3's further actions (direct claim, split, convert) are FUTURE BUILD and schema-rejected until implemented.

event_id.description: Stable identity of the defining Event; never changes across transitions (EVT2).

selected_outcome_id.description: Set exactly once at ACTIVE → RESOLVING; null before that.

EventInstance.description: One in-flight event's persistent state — the shape that lives in the SaveBlob `events` block (v3) so EVT3's save-atomicity claim is about the REAL save path, not a side file. `staged_effects` are inert copies made at ACTIVE → RESOLVING and preserved verbatim through RESOLVING → RESOLVED and across save/load: never executed, never duplicated, never dropped.

EventLifecycleState.description: Lifecycle state labels (Doc 15 §2; A3 brief §1.3). OFFERED and TRIGGERED are mutually exclusive paths — player-choice events offer, imposed events trigger; the slash notation in Doc 15's diagram expresses alternatives, never a literal combined state. RESOLVED, EXPIRED, and DECLINED are terminal.

Effect.description: Inert effect descriptor (Doc 15 §4): names the existing system it would route through. Carries NO amounts, NO payload, NO execution path — it is staged and preserved by the lifecycle, never applied (A3 brief §2).

EffectBinding.description: The existing system an effect WOULD bind to (Doc 15 §4 table). Pure reference vocabulary at A3: no dispatch to any of these systems exists, and the lifecycle never interprets this field (EVT5/EVT6 fail-loud).

next_expedition_index.description: Monotonic counter driving the deterministic expedition stream (brief §3).

last_command_id.description: The last applied command id — re-applying it is an idempotent no-op (brief §3).

ExpeditionState.description: The expedition domain state (SaveBlob v4 `expedition` block).

ExpeditionPhase.description:
Expedition lifecycle phase (brief §1/§2). A strict, deterministic sequence:

  idle → preparing → en_route → at_outpost → returning → docked        → (unloading is performed in-place at `docked`) → recovering? → idle

`cancelled` is not a phase: cancellation returns the domain to `idle` from `preparing` (OPS1 refund). Damage sends `docked`→`recovering`; a clean expedition returns straight to `idle`.

expedition_id.description: Deterministic identity `exp.<index>` from the seeded expedition stream (brief §3).

expedition_seed.description: Deterministic per-expedition seed derived from the stream (brief §3).

content_id.description: The version-pinned content id this expedition was prepared from (brief §3).

guardian_id.description: The selected starting Guardian (equivalent sidegrade — composition only).

supplies_committed.description: Supplies withdrawn from the Harbor at preparation and held on the vessel. Refunded exactly on cancellation (OPS1); consumed (spent) on dispatch.

dispatched.description: True once dispatched — supplies are then spent and cancellation is no longer legal.

outcome.description: The resolved outcome, or null until `at_outpost → returning`.

cargo_aboard.description: Recovered salvage still aboard the vessel, awaiting unload. Loaded at resolution per (outcome, guardian composition); reduced by each unload; any blocked remainder stays here (preserved, never dropped — brief §2.13).

event.description: The bounded at-outpost event (EVT1–EVT4 reuse). Null before arrival; created DORMANT at arrival and advanced deterministically to RESOLVED at resolution. Staged effects are inert (no execution).

ActiveExpedition.description: One in-flight expedition. Created at `prepare`, cleared at completion. All amounts are copied from the schema-validated /data expedition seed at preparation time (version-pinned content, brief §3) — never recomputed from magic numbers.

StartingGuardianId.description: The three starting Guardians (brief §2.3/§4). These ids match the canonical /data/guardians launch seeds (gdn.raxa / gdn.tarin / gdn.nova). The choice is an equivalent sidegrade — it selects salvage composition only.

ResourceAmounts.description: A partial per-CoreResource amount map (DC6: CoreResource keys only — never Merit/receipt metrics).

ExpeditionOutcome.description: The authorized outcome set (brief §2.9). `cancelled` is reachable only from `preparing` (pre-dispatch, full supply refund — OPS1); the other four are at-outpost resolutions selected at `at_outpost → returning`.

ReadinessCondition.description: Condition of the vessel / crew / Guardian after an outcome (brief §2.15 bounded recovery).

overflow.description: Unsafe Overflow holdings per CoreResource (brief §2.12): salvage that did not fit Safe Storage, capped per resource at `overflow_cap_multiplier ×` the current Safe capacity S. Never negative; never exceeds the cap.

canonical_intro_consumed.description: The one-time canonical intro (Theo's funeral / need surfacing, brief §2.1) has been consumed. Set true at the first `prepare`; repeat expeditions skip the narrative beat so they never replay a story contradiction (brief §2.17).

route_anchor_operations_unlocked.description: Limited route-anchor operations unlocked (brief §2.16) — set at first full success.

completed_expeditions.description: Count of completed expeditions (deterministic; supports repeatability, brief §2.17).

HarborOperationsState.description: Harbor-side A4 operations state (SaveBlob v4 `harbor_operations` block). Persists the unsafe Overflow holdings and the one-time unlock/intro flags.

EmptyMapM0.description: M0 empty-shell placeholder: schema-enforced empty map until the block's feature + migration land.

$comment: GENERATED from src/contracts/save-blob.ts (D39) — do not edit by hand; run `pnpm run schema:generate`.
